<L>44<pc>0006<k1>akza<k2>a/kza
{@ákṣa,@}¦ <ab>m.</ab>, {%Achse am Wagen%}. Die genaue Uebereinstimmung in der Form mit akṣa, Auge (<ab n="siehe">s.</ab> das folgende und <ab>vgl.</ab> ákṣi, Auge mit <lang>lat.</lang> axi-s) lässt noch immer die von <ls>Benfey (S. V. glo.)</ls> angegebene Erklärung, wonach die durch das Rad gesteckte Achse als Auge des Rades aufgefasst wurde, als die wahrscheinlichste erscheinen. <ab>Vgl.</ab> die Genetiven ráthasya, cakríos und die Zusammensetzung sam-akṣá.
<div n="TS">-a [<ab>V.</ab>] {287,19} (vīḍo vīḍita).
<div n="TS">-as {164,13}; {166,9}; {287,17}; {465,3}; {625,29}; {911,12} (ā́hatas).
<div n="TS">-am {30,14}. _{30,15}; {549,4}.
<div n="TS">-ena {915,4} (neben cakríyā).
<div n="TS">-e [<ab>L.</ab>] {666,27} (araṭvé).
<LEND>

<L>26<pc>0002-b<k1>akza<k2>akza
1. {#akza#}¦ {%akṣa,%} probably from a lost
<ab>vb.</ab> {%akṣ,%} corresponding to <lang n="greek">ὀσσ</lang> in <lang n="greek">ὄσσομαι</lang>
{@I.@} <ab>n.</ab> {@1.@} The eye; particularly
as latter part of <ab>comp.</ab> <ab>adj.</ab>, <ab>f.</ab> {%kṣī.%} {@2.@}
An organ of sense, <ls>Pañc.</ls> ii, <ab>d.</ab> 164.
{@II.@} <ab>m.</ab> {@1.@} A die, <ls>Man.</ls> 4, 74. {@2.@} The
name of a plant, Terminalia Bellerica,
of which the nuts are used as dice, and
the seed for making rosaries. {@ -- <ab>Comp.</ab>@}
{%Kamala-patra-,%} <ab>adj.</ab>, <ab>f.</ab> {%kṣī,%} <ab>adj.</ab> hav-
ing eyes like lotus-leaves. {%Kūṭa-,%} <ab>m.</ab> a
false die. {%Jāla-,%} <ab>m.</ab> a lattice window.
{%Tāmra-,%} <ab>adj.</ab> red-eyed. {%Tāraka-,%} 1. <ab>adj.</ab>
having stars for eyes. 2. <ab>m.</ab> a proper
name. {%Tryakṣa,%} <ab>i. e.</ab> {%tri-,%} {@I.@} <ab>adj.</ab> having
three eyes. {@II.@} <ab>m.</ab> a name of Rudra-
Śiva, and of a demon. {%Daśākṣa,%} <ab>i. e.</ab>
{%daśan-,%} <ab>m.</ab> the name of a spell or
charm {%Padma-,%} <ab>adj.</ab> lotus-eyed.
{%Puṇḍarīka-,%} <ab>m.</ab> a name of Viṣṇu.
{%Puṣkara-,%} <ab>adj.</ab> lotus-eyed. {%Manda-,%}
<ab>n.</ab> modesty. {%Lohita-,%} <ab>adj.</ab> red-eyed.
{%Sahasra-,%} {@I.@} <ab>adj.</ab> thousand-eyed. {@II.@} <ab>m.</ab>
a name of Indra. {%Su-,%} <ab>adj.</ab> 1. hand-
some-eyed. 2. acute. -- <ab>Cf.</ab> <lang n="greek">ὄσσομαι, ὄκκος</lang>;
<ab>Lat.</ab> oculus; and see {%akṣi.%}
<LEND>
